title: Running Switchboard and Swarm sync simultaneously triples operation storage to 3x
content:
When Connect runs the native SyncManager against a Switchboard AND the Swarm plugin at the same time, every operation is stored in three places:

```
CONNECT (browser)          SWITCHBOARD (server)       SWARM NETWORK
PGlite                     PostgreSQL                  /bytes + feeds
  - operations ----------> - operations
              <----------
                                                       - operation batches
  - operations ----------------------------------------> - manifests
```

**Storage cost**: 3x per operation (PGlite + PostgreSQL + Swarm).

**The upside is optional resilience**:
- Can recover from either Switchboard OR Swarm
- Team collaboration continues working via Switchboard
- Censorship-resistant backup via Swarm
- Useful during migration or for high-value data

**The cost is real**:
- Triple bandwidth for writes
- Triple stamp/storage cost for operations on Swarm
- Two sync paths to reason about for conflicts

This is Architecture C — not a target state but a useful interim for users who want both properties simultaneously, or while migrating off a Switchboard.
description: Architecture C (Connect + Switchboard + Swarm) stores every operation three times: PGlite, PostgreSQL, and Swarm /bytes — enabling recovery from either remote.
